title: Custom negative z values produce inverted Wilson intervals
reasoning: wilson exposes z as a caller-provided parameter but only validates k and n. Because z2 is squared while halfWidth keeps the original sign of z, passing a negative z makes halfWidth negative and returns lo greater than hi, violating the interval invariant and the documented lo/hi confidence-bound contract. The existing invalid-input tests cover k and n only, so this public parameter remains unchecked.
reproduction: Call wilson(5, 10, -1.96). The calculation produces the same center as the default case but a negative halfWidth, so lo becomes the default upper bound and hi becomes the default lower bound.
recommendation: Validate z before computing the interval, for example require Number.isFinite(z) and z >= 0, and throw RangeError for invalid z values.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The validation suite asserts RangeError for invalid k and n values, but it never passes a custom z value or checks the interval invariant when z is negative or non-finite.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add cases asserting wilson(5, 10, -1.96), wilson(5, 10, Number.NaN), and wilson(5, 10, Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY) throw RangeError, plus a valid custom z case if custom quantiles are supported.
minimumFixScope: packages/stats/src/main/stats.ts validation in wilson plus focused tests in packages/stats/src/test/stats.test.ts
